title: Ornithogalum rausii
taxonomy: Ornithogalum rausii is a species of plant that belongs to the family Asparagaceae. It is a type of flowering plant within the genus Ornithogalum. The classification of Ornithogalum rausii places it as an evergreen perennial bulbous plant. This classification is based on its characteristics, including its bulbous habit and the shape of its leaves. In terms of its taxonomy, Ornithogalum rausii is a part of the subtribe Glorinae.

intro: Ornithogalum rausii, also known as the Raus' Star-of-Bethlehem, is a flowering plant species in the Asparagaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ornithogalum rausii include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ornithogalum rausii is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
